category.name: Winter Wonderland
category.researchAlignment: Science connection - weather, states of matter, meteorology

questions.clue: This white, fluffy stuff falls from the sky in winter and is perfect for making snowmen
This frozen water forms when it's very cold outside and can be slippery to walk on
You wear these on your hands to keep them warm in the snow
This round object made of snow sits on top of a snowman's body
When you're outside in winter, you can see this coming out of your mouth when you breathe
These hang from the roof and are made of frozen water that drips and freezes again
This orange vegetable is used for a snowman's nose
You ride down a snowy hill on this flat piece of wood or plastic
This warm drink with marshmallows on top is perfect after playing in the snow
These special boots keep your feet warm and dry in the snow
You wrap this long, warm cloth around your neck in winter
Each one of these tiny pieces of snow has six points and looks like a tiny star
This is the season when snow falls and it gets very cold outside
These coal pieces are used to make buttons and eyes on a snowman
You put this hat on top of your head to keep warm in the snow
questions.correctQuestion: What is a winter hat (or beanie)?

questions.clue: When lots of snow falls all at once, we call it this kind of storm
These birds with orange bellies often stay during winter instead of flying south
You make these by lying in the snow and moving your arms and legs
This frozen rain falls as little balls of ice instead of snowflakes
People build these snow structures to have pretend battles throwing snowballs
When snow melts a little and then freezes again, it makes these slippery patches
questions.correctQuestion: What is black ice (or ice patches)?

questions.clue: This special coat keeps you extra warm and puffy in the winter
questions.correctQuestion: What is a puffer jacket (or winter coat)?

questions.clue: These animals with bushy tails gather nuts before winter and hide in trees
This word means when the temperature drops below the freezing point
These tiny ice crystals form on windows in pretty patterns when it's very cold
This frozen lake or pond is where people go ice skating in winter
questions.correctQuestion: What is a frozen pond (or ice rink)?

questions.clue: These special socks keep your toes warm and cozy in winter boots
questions.correctQuestion: What are wool socks (or thick socks)?

questions.clue: This house made entirely of ice blocks is where some people in very cold places live
When snow is perfect for making snowmen, we say it's this kind of snow
questions.correctQuestion: What is packing snow (or wet snow)?

questions.clue: These red birds with pointy heads stay in winter and stand out against white snow
This frozen waterfall happens when moving water freezes in the winter
questions.correctQuestion: What is an ice fall (or frozen waterfall)?

questions.clue: These special lights in the sky sometimes appear in winter in very cold places
questions.correctQuestion: What are the Northern Lights (or Aurora Borealis)?

questions.clue: This special day in December is the shortest day with the least sunlight of the whole year
This dangerous pile of snow can slide down a mountain very fast
These large sheets of ice float in the ocean in very cold places near the North Pole
This special kind of snow that looks like powder is very light and fluffy
Water freezes into ice at this temperature in degrees Celsius
This state of matter is what water becomes when it freezes
This process is when water vapor in clouds turns directly into ice crystals without becoming liquid first
This winter sport involves sliding down snowy mountains on two long boards attached to your feet
This type of cloud, often gray and low in the sky, usually brings snow in winter
All snowflakes have this many sides or points due to the hexagonal structure of ice crystals
This term describes frozen water droplets that fall as small, hard pellets
This instrument measures the temperature of the air outside
This layer of gas surrounds Earth and contains the weather we experience
This winter sport uses a single wide board to slide down snowy slopes
This scientist studies weather patterns and makes predictions about upcoming conditions
This happens when water changes from liquid to gas, like when puddles disappear after snow melts
This severe winter storm has winds over 35 mph and heavy snow reducing visibility
This type of precipitation forms when raindrops freeze into ice pellets before hitting the ground
This scale measures wind speed and is often used to describe blizzard conditions
This Japanese scientist photographed over 5,000 snowflakes and proved no two are exactly alike
questions.correctQuestion: Who is Wilson Bentley (or Snowflake Bentley)?

questions.clue: This protective layer of fat helps polar animals survive in freezing temperatures
This is the scientific term for when animals sleep through the winter to conserve energy
This happens when water vapor touches a cold surface and freezes directly into ice crystals
This Olympic winter sport involves sliding down an ice track on a small sled while lying face-down
This large mass of ice and snow that slowly moves down mountains is called this
This temperature scale is used in most scientific measurements and has water freezing at 0 degrees
This medical condition occurs when body tissue freezes due to extreme cold exposure
This phenomenon causes ice to float on water because frozen water is less dense than liquid water
This classification system identifies seven main types of snowflake shapes based on temperature and humidity
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Nakaya diagram (or snowflake classification)?

questions.clue: This weather phenomenon creates a visible ring or halo around the sun or moon during winter
This massive pile of snow that can rush down a mountain at over 80 mph is extremely dangerous
This layer of the atmosphere where weather occurs extends about 12 kilometers above Earth's surface
This type of snow crystal forms at temperatures between -10 and -22 degrees Celsius, creating plate-like shapes
This measurement describes how cold the air feels on exposed skin when factoring in wind speed
This process is when solid ice changes directly to water vapor without becoming liquid first
This instrument measures atmospheric pressure and helps predict weather changes
This rare weather event called 'thundersnow' combines lightning with this type of precipitation
This is the lowest temperature theoretically possible, equal to -273.15 degrees Celsius
This term describes the boundary between cold and warm air masses that often creates winter storms
questions.correctQuestion: What is a front (or cold front)?

questions.clue: This phenomenon occurs when supercooled water droplets instantly freeze on contact with surfaces
questions.correctQuestion: What is rime ice (or hoar frost)?

questions.clue: This effect describes how water's maximum density at 4 degrees Celsius allows aquatic life to survive winter
questions.correctQuestion: What is the density anomaly of water?

questions.clue: This atmospheric circulation pattern brings cold Arctic air southward during winter months
This ratio of water vapor present to the maximum possible at a given temperature affects snowflake formation
This branch of Earth science specifically studies atmospheric phenomena including winter weather
This term describes snow that has been compacted and recrystallized over multiple seasons
This scale categorizes blizzards from 1 to 5 based on their severity and impacts
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Regional Snowfall Index (or NESIS)?

questions.clue: This greenhouse gas, in the form of snow and ice, has high albedo that reflects solar radiation
This phenomenon describes the scattering of light by ice crystals that makes snow appear white
questions.correctQuestion: What is Mie scattering (or light diffusion)?

questions.clue: This ocean current helps moderate winter temperatures in Western Europe by bringing warm water north
This term describes the percentage of solar radiation reflected by a surface like snow or ice
This climate oscillation in the Pacific affects winter precipitation patterns across North America
This force, caused by Earth's rotation, deflects moving air and affects winter storm tracks
This type of cloud forms when moist air is forced upward over mountains, often producing heavy snow
This measurement equals the depth of water that would result from melting snow, typically 10:1 ratio
questions.correctQuestion: What is snow water equivalent (or SWE)?

questions.clue: This atmospheric instability creates intense localized snowfall called snow squalls near large bodies of water
This process occurs when falling snow partially melts, then refreezes into irregular ice pellets
questions.correctQuestion: What is graupel formation (or soft hail)?

questions.clue: This temperature inversion traps cold air near the surface while warmer air sits above
This atmospheric river phenomenon can bring heavy precipitation to the West Coast during winter
This Norwegian scientist developed the theory of cold and warm fronts that revolutionized meteorology
This crystalline structure of ice features hydrogen bonds arranged in a hexagonal lattice
questions.correctQuestion: What is ice Ih (or hexagonal ice)?

questions.clue: This weather phenomenon produces lightning and thunder during snowstorms due to strong convection
This measurement technique uses radar to estimate precipitation rates and track winter storms
This layer of permanently frozen ground beneath the surface affects Arctic and subarctic regions
This phenomenon creates snow rollers when wind rolls snow into cylindrical shapes across fields
This Japanese physicist created the first artificial snowflakes in a laboratory in 1936
This process describes how ice crystals grow at the expense of liquid water droplets in mixed-phase clouds
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Bergeron process (or Wegener-Bergeron-Findeisen process)?

questions.clue: This type of winter storm forms when cold continental air meets warm oceanic air along the East Coast
This climate feedback loop accelerates warming as decreased ice and snow cover reduces Earth's reflectivity
This stratospheric phenomenon involves sudden temperature increases that can affect winter weather patterns
questions.correctQuestion: What is sudden stratospheric warming (or SSW)?

questions.clue: This dimensionless number describes the ratio of thermal to kinetic energy in atmospheric flows
This boundary layer phenomenon creates temperature gradients that affect snow crystal morphology
questions.correctQuestion: What is the temperature gradient metamorphism?

questions.clue: This mesoscale meteorological feature produces intense snowbands in winter cyclones
questions.correctQuestion: What are frontogenetical circulations (or deformation zones)?

questions.clue: This theoretical framework explains snowflake symmetry through diffusion-limited aggregation models
This index quantifies the North Atlantic Oscillation that influences European and North American winters
This microphysical process involves ice crystal multiplication through secondary ice production
This climate mode involves stratospheric polar vortex variability affecting tropospheric circulation
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Arctic Oscillation (or AO)?

questions.clue: This 1888 storm paralyzed the Northeast United States, killing over 400 people in the Great White Hurricane
This climate pattern oscillating every 2-7 years significantly impacts winter precipitation globally
questions.correctQuestion: What is the El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO)?

questions.clue: This 1993 superstorm affected 26 states and caused over $6 billion in damage across Eastern North America
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Storm of the Century (or 1993 Superstorm)?

questions.clue: This Russian city holds the record for lowest temperature ever recorded in an inhabited place at -96°F
This atmospheric blocking pattern can cause prolonged cold spells by stalling weather systems
This location in Antarctica recorded Earth's coldest temperature of -128.6°F in 1983
This 1996 blizzard buried parts of the Eastern United States under several feet of snow, paralyzing major cities
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Blizzard of 1996 (or North American blizzard)?

questions.clue: This phenomenon occurs when Arctic sea ice extent reaches its annual minimum, typically in September
This U.S. state holds the record for the most snow in a single season at Mount Baker with 1,140 inches
This winter of 1816, known as the 'Year Without a Summer,' followed this Indonesian volcano's eruption
This meteorological service began issuing winter storm warnings in the United States in 1870
questions.correctQuestion: What is the National Weather Service (or Weather Bureau)?

questions.clue: This phenomenon describes the warming of Arctic regions at twice the global average rate
This 1967 Chicago blizzard dropped 23 inches of snow and stranded thousands of vehicles
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Chicago Blizzard of 1967?

questions.clue: This Canadian province experiences the most severe winter weather with temperatures below -40°F common
This teleconnection pattern links stratospheric circulation anomalies to surface winter weather
This 1972 blizzard in Iran killed approximately 4,000 people, making it the deadliest on record
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Iran Blizzard of 1972?

questions.clue: This period from roughly 1300-1850 CE featured cooler global temperatures and harsh winters
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Little Ice Age?

questions.clue: This winter of 1683-1684 saw the Thames River freeze so solidly that Londoners held a frost fair
This weather modification technique involves seeding clouds with silver iodide to enhance snowfall
This 2014 polar vortex event brought record cold to much of North America with wind chills below -50°F
questions.correctQuestion: What is the 2014 North American cold wave?

questions.clue: This 1978 nor'easter buried parts of New England under 27 inches of snow with 100 mph winds
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Northeastern United States blizzard of 1978?

questions.clue: This atmospheric index measures the pressure difference between Iceland and the Azores
questions.correctQuestion: What is the North Atlantic Oscillation (NAO)?

questions.clue: This 2010 snowstorm nicknamed 'Snowmageddon' paralyzed Washington D.C. with over 30 inches
questions.correctQuestion: What is the February 2010 North American blizzard?

questions.clue: This submarine ridge extending across the Arctic Ocean affects sea ice dynamics and winter circulation
This winter weather phenomenon involves downslope winds that can raise temperatures 40°F in hours
questions.correctQuestion: What is a chinook wind (or föhn wind)?

questions.clue: This 1880-1881 winter saw the Schoolhouse Blizzard kill 235 people across the Great Plains
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Schoolhouse Blizzard (or Children's Blizzard)?

questions.clue: This climate proxy uses oxygen isotope ratios in ice cores to reconstruct past winter temperatures
This 1950 Thanksgiving storm produced snow from the Appalachians to the Atlantic, killing 353 people
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Great Appalachian Storm of 1950?

questions.clue: This paleoclimate period around 12,800 years ago saw a rapid return to glacial conditions
This Norwegian explorer's 1893-1896 Arctic drift expedition provided crucial winter climate data
This 1940 Armistice Day Blizzard killed 154 people with sudden temperature drops of 50°F
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Armistice Day Blizzard?

questions.clue: This Greenland ice core project drilled 3,029 meters, revealing 110,000 years of climate history
questions.correctQuestion: What is GISP2 (Greenland Ice Sheet Project)?

questions.clue: This mechanism describes how Dansgaard-Oeschger events caused rapid warming during glacial periods
This 1779-1780 winter was the coldest on record for the Eastern United States with New York Harbor frozen
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Hard Winter (or Winter of 1780)?

questions.clue: This climate reconstruction method uses varved lake sediments to analyze historical winter conditions
This 1717 nor'easter buried New England under snow drifts up to 25 feet deep
questions.correctQuestion: What is the Great Snow of 1717?
